title: trifolium longipes subsp. elmeri
intro: Trifolium longipes subsp. elmeri is a subspecies of the Long-stalked Clover plant, a type of legume native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Trifolium longipes subsp. elmeri include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and even exhibit antibacterial properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
